data.title: Pantera and other investors pushed Satsuma to sell $50 million of its Bitcoin reserves.
data.description: Investors including Pantera Capital Management are pushing UK-based Bitcoin reserve company Satsuma to sell $50 million worth of its Bitcoin reserves. Satsuma, which shifted to an "AI-driven" Bitcoin reserve strategy in August 2025, successfully raised £164 million (approximately $221 million) through convertible loan notes, led by ParaFi Capital, with participation from Pantera, Digital Currency Group (DCG), Kraken, Arrington Capital, and others. Satsuma has confirmed that some shareholders are "demanding the return of capital," but has not disclosed the identities of these shareholders. Satsuma Executive Chairman Ranald McGregor-Smith stated in an emailed statement that the company is exploring options to facilitate these requests while protecting the interests of all shareholders. (The Block)

data.title: Cluster Protocol announced the completion of a $5 million funding round, led by DAO5.
data.description: Cluster Protocol, an AI deep learning and Web3 infrastructure project, announced the completion of a $5 million funding round led by DAO5, with participation from Paper Ventures, JPEG Trading, Mapleblock Capital, and others. To date, the company has raised a total of $77.5 million. The funds will primarily be used to accelerate the development of CodeXero, an IDE designed specifically for the EVM ecosystem. This IDE supports the browser's native Vibe Coding experience, allowing developers to directly transform their ideas into deployable smart contracts through a single authoring environment, significantly lowering the barrier to entry for Web3 development. (TheBlock)

data.title: Cluster Protocol Secures $5 Million in Funding to Enhance Web3 Development
data.description: AI deep technology and Web3 infrastructure project Cluster Protocol has announced the completion of a $5 million funding round. According to Odaily, DAO5 led the investment, with participation from Paper Ventures, JPEG Trading, and Mapleblock Capital. The company's total funding has now reached $77.5 million. The newly acquired funds will primarily be used to accelerate the development of CodeXero, an IDE specifically designed for the EVM ecosystem. CodeXero supports browser-native Vibe Coding experiences, enabling developers to transform ideas into deployable smart contracts within a single creative environment, significantly lowering the barriers to Web3 development.

data.title: MoonPay Launches Virtual Account Services in New York
data.description:
MoonPay has announced the launch of its Iron platform's virtual account services in New York State. According to Foresight News, this service supports fintech companies, crypto platforms, neobanks, and financial institutions in integrating compliant fiat-to-stablecoin infrastructure.

The launch is based on MoonPay's acquisition of the enterprise-level stablecoin infrastructure platform, Iron, in 2025. Users can receive funds through ACH, wire transfers, and SWIFT, which are automatically converted into stablecoins and settled into non-custodial wallets. In the same year, MoonPay received a BitLicense, money transmitter license, and limited purpose trust license from the New York State Department of Financial Services.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Russia's Central Bank Gold and Forex Reserves Rise to $779.5 Billion
data.description: Russia's central bank reported an increase in its gold and foreign exchange reserves, reaching $779.5 billion for the week ending April 17. According to Jin10, this marks a rise from the previous value of $774.8 billion. The growth in reserves reflects ongoing adjustments in Russia's financial strategy.

data.title: Analysis: With US blockade measures in place, shipping in the Strait of Hormuz is nearly at a standstill.
data.description: Traffic in the Strait of Hormuz remains largely at a standstill, with only occasional passage of vessels linked to Iran. According to ship tracking data, as of Thursday morning local time, only one large cargo ship with Iranian ties was observed departing from the Persian Gulf. The security situation in the strait deteriorated further on Wednesday when Iranian warships opened fire on merchant ships, and the United States intercepted two oil tankers from Tehran. (Jinshi)

data.title: Tensions Rise in Hormuz Strait Amid Iranian Naval Activity
data.description: Traffic in the Hormuz Strait remains largely stagnant, with occasional passage of vessels linked to Iran. According to BlockBeats, ship tracking data as of Thursday morning local time indicated that only one large cargo ship associated with Iran was observed departing from the Persian Gulf. On Wednesday, the security situation in the strait worsened when Iranian warships fired upon a commercial vessel, while the United States intercepted two oil tankers from Tehran.

data.title: Multiple Kasplex ecosystem meme projects have seen significant growth.
data.description: The Kasplex L2 ecosystem's Meme tokens have seen significant overall growth, with several reaching new market capitalization highs. KAS.FUN data shows that among the graduated projects, Sompi currently has a market capitalization of $25 million, boobot $3.4 million, and JNSO $525,000. Previously, it was reported that the Kasplex L2 network announced its integration with Gate on April 17th, opening up liquidity access to centralized exchanges.

data.title: Both WTI and Brent crude oil prices rose by $1 in the short term after Trump stated that he had ordered the sinking of any ships laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: According to Gate data, both WTI and Brent crude oil prices rose by $1 in the short term. Trump stated that he had ordered the Navy to sink any ships that laid mines in the Strait of Hormuz, and that all 159 Iranian naval vessels had been sunk.

data.title: Coinbase and other crypto institutions jointly wrote to the US Senate urging the passage of the CLARITY Act.
data.description: The Blockchain Association and CCI, along with over 120 industry organizations including Coinbase, Ripple, Kraken, and Circle, jointly wrote to the U.S. Senate Banking Committee, urging them to expedite the review process of the Clarity Act. The industry representatives stated that the U.S. needs to establish a unified regulatory framework for the digital asset market, clarifying regulatory responsibilities and strengthening investor protection; relying solely on enforcement and regulation cannot create a long-term stable environment. The organizations warned that prolonged policy ambiguity will lead to the outflow of capital, talent, and technology, weakening the U.S.'s strategic advantage. They also called for the development of suitable, unified federal regulatory rules for stablecoins, tokenized assets, and decentralized technologies.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Orders Navy to Target Iranian Vessels in Strait of Hormuz
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced that he has instructed the Navy to sink any vessels laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz. According to Foresight News, Trump stated that the Navy's minesweepers are currently clearing the strait. He further claimed that all 159 Iranian naval vessels have been sunk.

data.title: AVAX One Reports Doubling Revenue and Plans New Data Center
data.description:
AVAX One, a Nasdaq-listed company, has announced its preliminary results for the first quarter of 2026. According to Odaily, the company's quarterly revenue doubled to approximately $2.4 million, driven by growth in Avalanche staking rewards and Bitcoin mining operations. In terms of asset allocation, AVAX One holds around 14 million AVAX tokens, with over 90% staked.

The company has also signed a letter of intent to build a 10MW Tier 3 AI/HPC data center in Alberta, Canada. This project is expected to be operational in the first quarter of 2027 and will leverage low-cost natural gas power resources. The initiative aims to advance a dual strategy of 'crypto asset revenue + computing infrastructure' to support AI and digital assets.

Additionally, AVAX One has expanded its Bitcoin mining capacity by adding 220 new machines, increasing its computing power to over 200 PH/s.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | International Spot Gold Lacks Bullish Momentum
data.description: International spot gold is experiencing insufficient bullish momentum, with rebound highs continuously declining. According to Jin10, before confirming a breakout, the market may continue to fluctuate downward.

data.title: Bitmine deposited 75,600 ETH into Ethereum's PoS staking pool, worth $176 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ember, after adding 100,000 ETH this afternoon, Bitmine just deposited 75,600 ETH ($176 million) into its Ethereum PoS staking. Currently, Bitmine has staked a total of 3.471 million ETH, representing 70% of their total ETH holdings (4.976 million ETH).

data.title: Bitcoin Rewards Program Launched for Employees by Fold
data.description:
Bitcoin financial services company Fold has announced a new employee Bitcoin rewards program, according to Foresight News. This initiative allows employers to offer periodic Bitcoin rewards with a built-in vesting mechanism, without needing to alter payroll systems or assume custody or compliance responsibilities. Fold manages the transfer, custody, and vesting of Bitcoin.

Steak 'n Shake has partnered with Fold to enable employees to earn Bitcoin as part of their compensation. Additionally, Bitcoin mining hosting company Simple Mining is utilizing the rewards program to distribute Bitcoin rewards based on employee tenure.

data.title: AVAX One announced its Q1 results: holding approximately 14 million AVAX tokens, revenue more than doubled quarter-over-quarter.
data.description: AVAX One, a Nasdaq-listed AVAX treasury company, announced preliminary results for the first quarter of 2026. Benefiting from Avalanche staking rewards and growth in Bitcoin mining, quarterly revenue more than doubled sequentially to approximately $2.4 million. Regarding asset allocation, the company currently holds approximately 14 million AVAX tokens, with over 90% pledged. Simultaneously, AVAX One has signed a letter of intent to build a 10MW Tier 3 AI/HPC data center in Alberta, Canada. The project is expected to be operational in the first quarter of 2027, leveraging low-cost natural gas and electricity resources to advance its dual-track strategy of "crypto asset rewards + computing infrastructure" for AI and digital assets. The company also added 220 Bitcoin mining rigs, increasing its hashrate to over 200 PH/s. (globenewswire)

data.title: U.S. Weekly Jobless Claims Average Revised to 210,750
data.description: The four-week average of initial jobless claims in the United States reached 210,750 for the week ending April 18, according to Jin10. The previous value was revised from 209,750 to 210,000.

data.title: Canada's March Industrial Product Price Index Exceeds Expectations
data.description: Canada's Industrial Product Price Index for March showed a monthly increase of 2.4%, surpassing the anticipated 1.9%, according to Jin10. The previous month's rate was recorded at 0.40%.

data.title: A senior Iranian presidential official said reaching an agreement with the US "will take time."
data.description: Seyed Abbas Mousavi, deputy chief of staff of the Iranian president, told the media that reaching an agreement with the United States would take time. Mousavi said that it should not be forgotten that Iran is currently at war, and that Iran is not facing trade negotiations, but rather "negotiations with an enemy." Iran needs time to fight for its rights and uphold its position. He said that negotiations "should not be rushed," and that it should be understood that Iran needs to safeguard its national interests and security based on full internal coordination. (Xinhua)

data.title: Iranian Official States Time Needed for U.S. Agreement
data.description: Iran's Deputy Chief of Staff for the President, Seyed Abbas Mousavi, stated that reaching an agreement with the United States requires time. According to Odaily, Mousavi emphasized that Iran is currently in a state of conflict, and the negotiations are not typical trade discussions but rather talks with an adversary. He highlighted the importance of Iran striving for its rights and maintaining its stance, which necessitates time. Mousavi advised against rushing the negotiation process, stressing the need for Iran to safeguard its national interests and security through thorough internal coordination.
